feature_summary: security: same-origin trust break and credential leakage through /proxy/* external app proxy - header stripping, error response sanitization, UI warning banner
acceptance_criteria: SENSITIVE_REQUEST_HEADERS and SENSITIVE_RESPONSE_HEADERS constants added to src/lib/proxy/config.ts
proxyHttp() strips sensitive request headers (cookie, authorization, proxy-authorization, x-forwarded-for, x-forwarded-host, x-forwarded-proto, x-real-ip) before forwarding to upstream

proxyHttp() strips sensitive response headers (set-cookie, content-security-policy, content-security-policy-report-only, x-frame-options, strict-transport-security, and 6 CORS access-control-* headers) from upstream responses
proxyWebSocket() 426 response no longer includes directUrl field and message field does not contain internal URL information
route.ts catch block returns fixed-string error message (PROXY_ERROR_MESSAGES.BAD_GATEWAY) instead of (error as Error).message
route.ts 404 response does not include pathPrefix information
route.ts 503 response does not include app.displayName information
ExternalAppForm.tsx displays a security warning banner about same-origin risks
IProxyHandler.proxyWebSocket() JSDoc updated to reflect directUrl removal
Existing tests in tests/unit/proxy/handler.test.ts updated to reflect new stripping behavior
New tests added for request header stripping, response header stripping, safe header forwarding, and directUrl/message removal verification
CLAUDE.md handler.ts and config.ts module descriptions updated with Issue #395 security hardening details
npm run lint passes with 0 errors
npm run test:unit passes with all tests passing
test_scenarios: Scenario 1: Verify SENSITIVE_REQUEST_HEADERS constant exists in config.ts with 7 headers (cookie, authorization, proxy-authorization, x-forwarded-for, x-forwarded-host, x-forwarded-proto, x-real-ip)
Scenario 2: Verify SENSITIVE_RESPONSE_HEADERS constant exists in config.ts with 11 headers including set-cookie, CSP headers, security headers, CORS headers
Scenario 3: Verify proxyHttp() does not forward cookie header to upstream
Scenario 4: Verify proxyHttp() does not forward authorization header to upstream
Scenario 5: Verify proxyHttp() does not forward proxy-authorization header to upstream
Scenario 6: Verify proxyHttp() does not forward x-forwarded-for header to upstream
Scenario 7: Verify proxyHttp() does not forward x-forwarded-host header to upstream
Scenario 8: Verify proxyHttp() does not forward x-forwarded-proto header to upstream
Scenario 9: Verify proxyHttp() does not forward x-real-ip header to upstream
Scenario 10: Verify proxyHttp() still forwards safe headers like Content-Type, Accept, User-Agent
Scenario 11: Verify proxyHttp() strips set-cookie from upstream response
Scenario 12: Verify proxyHttp() strips content-security-policy from upstream response
Scenario 13: Verify proxyHttp() strips content-security-policy-report-only from upstream response
Scenario 14: Verify proxyHttp() strips x-frame-options from upstream response
Scenario 15: Verify proxyHttp() strips strict-transport-security from upstream response
Scenario 16: Verify proxyHttp() strips all CORS access-control-* headers from upstream response
Scenario 17: Verify proxyWebSocket() 426 response does not contain directUrl field
Scenario 18: Verify proxyWebSocket() 426 response message does not contain host/port information
Scenario 19: Verify ExternalAppForm.tsx shows amber security warning banner
Scenario 20: Verify route.ts 404 does not include pathPrefix in error message
Scenario 21: Verify route.ts 503 does not include app.displayName in error message
Scenario 22: Verify route.ts catch block does not include (error as Error).message in response
Scenario 23: Run npm run test:unit - all tests pass
Scenario 24: Run npx tsc --noEmit - no TypeScript errors
Scenario 25: Run npm run lint - no ESLint errors
